2026-04-23T07:08:07.8749013Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8749585Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8750016Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8750384Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8750697Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8750931Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8751084Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8751258Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8751423Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8751648Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8751844Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8752120Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:08:07.8752737Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-23T07:11:16.0143739Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T07:09:01.871718771Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58558,\"title\":\"[DevOpsInfrastructure] Docs: Add service-specific key concepts and code examples to README for GA release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58546 (commit 6bc663352) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58546 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` from `1.0.0-beta.1` to its first stable GA release `1.0.0`. The CHANGELOG entry for `1.0.0` only notes dependency upgrades — it does not describe the service\\u0026#39;s resources or capabilities. The README is still a boilerplate copy of the ARM template with no service-specific content or code samples.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Key concepts` section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK docs; it does not explain the core resources (`DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsPoolCollection`) or the key abstractions (organization profiles for Azure DevOps vs GitHub, agent profiles for stateless/stateful agents, VM fabric profiles)\\n- The `Examples` section contains only a link to a generic samples page — no inline code demonstrating how to create, list, get, or delete a `DevOpsPool`\\n- Four test sample files exist in `tests/Samples/` (`Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, `Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`) but none contain `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README\\n- The CHANGELOG for `1.0.0` describes no features for end users of the stable release (it only mentions dependency bumps from the beta)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the boilerplate `Key concepts` section (currently just a link) with service-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` library lets you manage **Managed DevOps Pools** — fully managed Azure DevOps agent pools backed by Azure virtual machines.\\n\\n### Core resources\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `DevOpsPoolResource` | Represents a single Managed DevOps Pool. |\\n| `DevOpsPoolCollection` | Collection of pools scoped to a resource group. |\\n\\n### Organization profiles\\n\\nPools can serve either Azure DevOps organizations (`DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`) or GitHub organizations (`DevOpsGitHubOrganizationProfile`).\\n\\n### Agent profiles\\n\\n- `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` — agents are fresh for each job (recommended for most workloads).\\n- `DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile` — agents retain state across jobs for faster warm-up.\\n- `AutomaticResourcePredictionsProfile` — the service automatically predicts and pre-provisions agents.\\n\\n### Fabric profiles\\n\\n`DevOpsVmssFabricProfile` specifies the VM SKU, OS images, network configuration, and OS settings used for the pool\\u0026#39;s virtual machines.\\n\\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the boilerplate `Examples` section with snippet placeholders:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Managed DevOps Pool\\n\\n````C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### List pools in a resource group\\n\\n````\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Get a pool by name\\n\\n````C# Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Delete a pool\\n\\n````\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite a new test sample file at:\\n`sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\nUse the operations already demonstrated in `Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs` and `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs` as the basis. The snippet names must match the README placeholders above.\\n\\n```\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class Sample_HelloWorld\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateDevOpsPool()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n            string poolName = \\u0026#34;myPool\\u0026#34;;\\n            DevOpsPoolData data = new DevOpsPoolData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                Properties = new DevOpsPoolProperties(\\n                    maximumConcurrency: 4,\\n                    organizationProfile: new DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile(new[]\\n                    {\\n                        new DevOpsOrganization(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(dev.azure.com/redacted) { OpenAccess = true }\\n                    }),\\n                    agentProfile: new DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile(),\\n                    fabricProfile: new DevOpsVmssFabricProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D2ads_v5\\u0026#34;, new[]\\n                    {\\n                        new DevOpsPoolVmImage { ResourceId = \\u0026#34;/MicrosoftWindowsServer/WindowsServer/2022-datacenter/latest\\u0026#34; }\\n                    }),\\n                    devCenterProjectResourceId: \\u0026#34;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.DevCenter/projects/myProject\\u0026#34;)\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await collection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, poolName, data);\\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool created: {pool.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListDevOpsPools()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n            await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool: {pool.Data.Name}, Location: {pool.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetDevOpsPool()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = await resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPoolAsync(\\u0026#34;myPool\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool name: {pool.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteDevOpsPool()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\\n\\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = await resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPoolAsync(\\u0026#34;myPool\\u0026#34;);\\n            await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Pool deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` currently has all required sections, but the following need content updates:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — missing; replace generic link with service-specific content (see Step 1)\\n2. **Examples** — missing inline code; replace generic link with snippet placeholders (see Step 1)\\n\\nAll other sections (Getting started, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present and adequate.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — injects `#region Snippet:` code from the new test file into README.md placeholders. Check `git diff` afterwards to confirm README was updated.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — regenerates API listing files (no public API change expected, but run to confirm)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateDevOpsPool()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\\n\\n            DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\\n\\n            string poolName = \\u0026#34;myPool\\u0026#34;;\\n            DevOpsPoolData data = new DevOpsPoolData(new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n DevOpsPoolVmImage { ResourceId = \\u0026#34;/MicrosoftWindowsServer/WindowsServer/2022-datacenter/latest\\u0026#34; }\\n                    }),\\n                    devCenterProjectResourceId: \\u0026#34;/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.DevCenter/projects/myProject\\u0026#34;)\\n            };\\n\\n            ArmOperation lro = await collection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, poolName, data);\\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool created: {pool.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task pool in collection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Pool: {pool.Data.Name}, Location: {pool.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteDevOpsPool()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at
